commit.message:
Fit all windows within 600 px height for small Haiku screens

- Reduced ExtractionWindow tips scroll area: 130→70 min, 180→90 max
- Reduced RoastColorWindow thumbnail: 320×240 → 320×180
- Reduced RoastColorWindow tips scroll: 130→70 min, 200→100 max
- Removed unnecessary 40px height buffer from all three windows' ResizeTo() calls
- Updated BRIEF.md to reflect thumbnail size changes and window optimization

All tool windows now fit within 600px total height (content + title bar + borders)
to accommodate smaller Haiku displays.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add PNG icon build step and update documentation

- set_icon.cpp: Haiku helper that scales toolkit.png to 32×32/16×16,
  converts to BeOS CMAP8 palette and writes BEOS:APP_ICON/BEOS:MINI_ICON
  file attributes so Tracker displays the icon
- coffee_toolkit.rdef: remove broken VICN/PNG resource (HVIF ≠ PNG bytes)
- Makefile: add set_icon build rule, mimeset call, and icon attribute step
- README.md: mark Particle Analyzer complete, fix manual build command,
  update project structure, update license section
- BRIEF.md: update file structure and build sections to match

commit.message:
fix: rename GlobalError→RouteError in error.tsx + suppress S6759

- app/error.tsx: rename export to RouteError to avoid duplicate name
  with global-error.tsx (both previously exported GlobalError)
- sonar-project.properties: suppress typescript:S6759 on frontend/**
  (error boundary prop destructuring is required by Next.js contract)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add --no-install-recommends to apt-get install (docker:S6500)

Reduces image attack surface by not installing unnecessary recommended
packages alongside gcc and libpq-dev in the backend Docker image.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

merge: integrate main (CAE sub-tabs + Railway overhaul) into sanarQubIssuesFIxes

Conflicts resolved:
- backend/Dockerfile: combined non-root user security fix with start.sh entrypoint,
  merged consecutive RUN instructions into one layer
- frontend/components/modules/cae-tab.tsx: took main's rewrite (sub-tabs + useToast),
  our alert() fix was already absorbed
- PROGRESS.md: manual merge, appended Etape 14

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add type-check script and suppress S1128 for app pages

- Add `type-check` npm script (tsc --noEmit) to frontend/package.json
  to catch type-import regressions like the Phase 8 RequiredFieldsForm/CalendarView incident
- Suppress typescript:S1128 (unused imports) on frontend/app/** in sonar-project.properties
  to avoid false positives on JSX component imports that SonarCloud misidentifies as type-only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): rebuild HEMesh after push-pull so face splitting works

After push-pull creates/modifies geometry via Manifold, the HEMesh
becomes stale. Now we rebuild it from the new BufferGeometry so that
line-tool face splitting works on push-pull-created boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): build HEMesh directly at box creation, avoid lossy conversion

The round-trip BoxGeometry → HEMesh → BufferGeometry was corrupting
geometry. Now boxes are created with HEMesh as the source of truth,
and LineTool only attempts face splitting on objects that already
have an HEMesh (no more ensureHEMesh conversion).

add automation hooks for session, commit, edit, and notification events

feat(ui): replace skill template chips/spinner with Gallery dialog

Previously templates accumulated as chips (Portal) or Spinner entries (Android)
with no upper bound. Replaced with a dedicated Gallery dialog that shows each
template as a card with icon, author, updatedAt, and env-var status.

Web Portal:
- Single 'Browse Templates…' button opens a searchable gallery dialog
- After selecting a template the selected-row indicator appears with a 'Change' button
- Gallery filter input allows searching by label
- showRequiredVarsDialog Skip/Save now restore the correct edit context via _skillDialogCtx

Android:
- Spinner removed; replaced with 'Browse Templates…' MaterialButton
- Selected template shown in a compact row with a 'Change' button
- showTemplateGalleryDialog() inflates item_template_gallery.xml per template
- SkillTemplate data class gains author + updatedAt fields (JSON already included them)

ci: add .eslintrc.json to fix non-interactive lint in CI

next lint prompts interactively when no ESLint config exists,
which fails in GitHub Actions. Adding .eslintrc.json with
next/core-web-vitals preset fixes this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add independent Python L1 reference agent (no SDK)

Pure Python implementation (~120 lines, stdlib only) of the CKP L1
wire protocol. First non-TypeScript CKP implementation — shares zero
code with the SDK, proving the protocol is language-agnostic and
implementable from the spec alone. 13/13 L1 CONFORMANT.

CI and release-gate now run L1 conformance against the Python agent.

test(data): add test suite for cirra-ai-sf-data validators (#57)

* feat(audit): add default report template with brand tokens and styling guide

Adds assets/report-template.md as the single source of truth for HTML, Word,
and Excel report styling. Covers brand colours, severity palettes, CSS classes,
docx-js heading/table patterns, openpyxl header fills, and placeholder reference.
SKILL.md Phase 9 now references this template.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(audit): treat report template as default, not absolute; ask user to review

- Template header clarifies user-provided templates take precedence
- SKILL.md Phase 9 notes user templates override the default
- Post-generation step asks user if they want to adjust style/structure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(audit): add self-contained report generator pipeline

- scripts/generate_reports.py: deterministic HTML/DOCX/XLSX/JSON report
  generator that reads scored JSON input and applies brand template
- docs/INPUT_SCHEMA.md: documents the JSON contract between AI agent and
  report generator (10 input files, all optional)
- tests/test_generate_reports.py: 13 smoke tests covering input loading,
  score computation, HTML generation, XSS escaping, and empty-data handling
- tests/fixtures/minimal/: complete set of fixture JSONs for testing
- SKILL.md Phase 9 updated to reference the generator script

Skipped from Codex proposal (doesn't fit MCP architecture):
- extract_data.sh / run_audit.sh (AI agent orchestrates via MCP, not CLI)
- build_inventory_extras.py (folded into generate_reports.py)
- RUNBOOK.md / QUERY_SPEC.md (SKILL.md already serves these roles)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(audit): add CLI-based extraction script for local mode

scripts/extract_data.sh pulls all inventory counts, raw query results, and
bulk metadata source (Apex, Triggers, Flows, LWC) in one pass via sf CLI.
Only used when EXEC_MODE=local; cloud mode continues to use MCP tools.

Key query rules per Codex spec:
- Process Builder count uses Flow with ProcessType='Workflow'
- WorkflowRule query avoids non-existent Active field
- PermissionSet filtered: IsOwnedByProfile=false, NamespacePrefix=null, Type!='Group'

SKILL.md updated with local mode shortcut reference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(audit): v2.0 — phased architecture, incremental audits, sfdx-repo mode

Major SKILL.md rewrite:
- Phases renamed: A (Quick Pass), B (User Approval Gate), C (Deep Dive),
  D (Reports), E (Summary)
- New sfdx-repo mode: reads metadata from local DX project (fastest)
- Incremental audit support: detect delta via git log or LastModifiedDate,
  carry forward unchanged scores, track changed/new/removed components
- audit_state.md: persistent state file for resume across context compactions
- Generated/skip list: auto-skip MetadataService, fflib_*, etc.
- User approval gate before Deep Dive (cost transparency)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(audit): move report template from assets/ to references/

references/ is more semantically correct — it's a reference document
the agent consults, not a visual asset like the icons.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(audit): update comments to reference references/ after template move

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: exclude tests/ and fixtures/ from skill and plugin zips

- package-all.sh: add */tests/* and */fixtures/* to EXCLUDE_PATTERNS
- package-skills.sh: use rsync with --exclude='tests' --exclude='fixtures'
  instead of cp -r when copying skill to tmp dir for zipping
- package.sh (LWC): add same exclusions for plugin zips
- Bump cirra-ai-sf-audit version to 2.1.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(audit): move input schema from docs/ to references/

Rename INPUT_SCHEMA.md → references/report-input-schema.md. This is a
developer reference for the report generator, not user-facing documentation.
Remove the now-empty docs/ directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(data): add 111-test suite for cirra-ai-sf-data validators

Tests cover all three validators in the data skill:
- SOQLValidator: syntax errors, WHERE/LIMIT/ORDER BY detection, hardcoded
  IDs, indexed fields, comment handling, complexity analysis, optimization
  suggestions (30 tests)
- MCPDataValidator: soql_query params, sobject_dml operations, WHERE syntax
  warnings, PII detection, unsupported tool rejection, result structure
  (39 tests)
- DataOperationValidator: SOQL/Apex/CSV/JSON file scoring, category
  breakdowns, PII in files, score arithmetic (42 tests)

Mirrors the test structure from cirra-ai-sf-flow/tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(data): add MCP integration test protocol for live org validation

22-step end-to-end test protocol covering:
- Object describe, SOQL queries (basic, WHERE, relationships, aggregates)
- DML operations (insert, update, upsert with external ID, delete)
- Negative tests (missing required field, invalid field, malformed SOQL)
- Pre-flight validator checks (missing params, PII detection, invalid ops)
- Bulk operations (201 records crossing batch boundary, pagination)
- Full cleanup procedure

Run interactively in Claude Code with MCP access, similar to the flow
skill's test_flow_mcp_integration.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(data): add MCP integration test results from live org run

All 22 steps passed against admin@demo2.cirra.ai.dev sandbox.
Key finding: MCP server enforces 200-record limit per sobject_dml call.
Updated protocol with batching instructions, pagination patterns,
and documented error handling observations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(data): update skill for 200-record MCP limit and recordIds delete param

Findings from live integration testing revealed several inaccuracies:

- MCPDataValidator: add 200-record limit check (errors on > 200),
  handle delete's recordIds param, warn on legacy records-based delete
- SKILL.md: fix all references claiming 200+ records per call works,
  document recordIds for delete, fix default query limit (100 not 1000),
  add EXCEEDED_ID_LIMIT to error tables, add pagination guidance
- Tests: add TC-MD3b, TC-MD12, TC-MD13, TC-MD14 for new validations,
  update TC-MD3 and TC-MD9 for recordIds behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(audit): address Bugbot findings — JSON escaping, threshold alignment, item max_score

1. extract_data.sh: Replace heredoc JSON with python3 json.dump to
   safely handle special characters in org name/id/instance values
2. generate_reports.py: Align SCORE_THRESHOLDS to template spec
   (80/70 boundaries, not 90/75)
3. generate_reports.py: Use item's max_score in _recommendations_html
   instead of hardcoded domain max, matching compute_domain_score behavior
4. test_generate_reports.py: Update threshold boundary tests to match

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(audit): use <strong> instead of markdown ** in HTML recommendations

Bugbot correctly identified that **bold** markdown syntax renders as
literal asterisks inside raw HTML <div> tags. Replaced with <strong>.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(data): add 200-record limit check for legacy delete path

The backward-compat delete path (using records[] instead of recordIds[])
was missing the >200 record limit check that all other DML paths enforce.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review feedback across multiple files

- extract_data.sh: log warnings on query failure instead of silencing stderr
- generate_reports.py: add encoding="utf-8" to file reads, avoid mutating

- report-template.md: align severity/score colour thresholds to 80/70/60/40,

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Remove unused `summary` param from `_recommendations_html` (Bugbot)
- Remove unused `SUPPORTED_TOOLS` constant from mcp_validator.py
- Remove unused `MCPOperationValidator` backward-compat alias

commit.message:
feat: merge embedded-dev toolchain into code-server, remove embedded-dev service

All embedded-dev tools are now built directly into the code-server image,
enabling full embedded C/C++ development from the VS Code integrated terminal
without any docker exec indirection.

Added to Dockerfile.code-server:
- QEMU: qemu-user-static, qemu-system-arm, qemu-system-misc, binfmt-support
- Hardware debug: stlink-tools, libftdi1-dev, libhidapi-dev, libusb-1.0-0-dev
- Google Test: libgtest-dev + cmake/make install from source
- Build systems: scons
- Python tools: pyocd, esptool, pyusb, pycparser, cffi, conan
- Docs: sphinx, sphinx-rtd-theme, breathe, exhale
- Aliases: qemu-arm, flash-openocd, flash-pyocd, esp-flash, sphinx-init

Removed:
- docker/Dockerfile.embedded
- scripts/embedded-bashrc.sh, embedded-clang-format, embedded-vimrc
- embedded-dev service from docker-compose.yml and dev-toolchains volume
- All embedded-dev references from manage.sh, manage.ps1, test-docker-build.sh
- images/embedded-dev-env_latest.tar

Docs updated: README.md, QUICKSTART.md, MANIFEST.md, EXTENSIONS.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

current lb weight, calculates the plate setup, and animates when the
slider changes — grounding the numbers in physical gym reality.

commit.message:
Track E: decompressZstdWF basic specs — base case and single-frame equivalence (#797)

* feat: decompressZstdWF basic specs — base case and single-frame equivalence

Add Zip/Spec/ZstdFrame.lean with the first specifications for the
top-level Zstd decompressor entry point:

- decompressZstdWF_base: when pos ≥ data.size, returns accumulated
  output unchanged (recursion base case)
- decompressZstdWF_single_standard_frame: when input contains exactly
  one standard Zstd frame, returns output ++ decompressed content

Closes #790

fix(ace): starvation protection — bypass WR/momentum penalty at CONF_THRESHOLD floor

When WR/momentum factors reduce adj_conf below env_floor (CONF_THRESHOLD)
but raw_confidence passes the floor, use raw_confidence instead.
Prevents deadlock where low WR makes ALL signals untradeable permanently.

Root cause: WR=26.6% → calibration_wr_factor=0.95, recent cold streak →
momentum_factor=0.90 → adj_conf=0.57*0.95*0.90=0.487 < 0.54 floor.
Bot has been placing 0 real bets since 2026-03-04 due to ACE starvation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: compress decompressBlocksWF proof boilerplate with split/next pattern

with the 1-line `split at h; next => exact nomatch h` idiom across both
decompressBlocksWF_output_size_ge and decompressBlocksWF_pos_gt. This
eliminates 37 lines of error-case boilerplate while preserving the same
proof structure and readability.

Document that `split at h; next => exact nomatch h` is the correct
one-liner for batching error-case elimination, and that the seemingly
obvious `<;> try exact nomatch h` fails because nomatch emits
elaboration errors that try doesn't catch.

element no longer creates a stacking context — lightbox overlay works correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

claude: fix message handler lost on reconnect, add logging, fix DM names in !chats

Root cause: when WhatsApp disconnects and reconnects, connectToWhatsApp()
creates a new socket but message handlers were only registered on the
original socket. After any reconnection, the bot silently stopped
receiving messages.

Fix: persistent callback arrays in connection.ts that are re-registered
on every new socket. Also adds message/command logging for diagnostics
and uses getChatName() in !chats for DM contact name resolution.

commit.message:
test(context_assembler): fix non-git-repo test on macOS CI runners

On macOS x86 GitHub Actions runners (macos-15-intel), TMPDIR can resolve
to a path inside the runner work tree, causing `git status` in a tempdir
to succeed by discovering a parent repo.  The assertion
ctx.git_status_summary.is_none() then fails because git returns a clean
status for the parent repo rather than the expected "not a git repository".

Fix: acquire ENV_LOCK (as other env-sensitive tests do) and set
GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES to the workspace path before running assemble().
This prevents git from traversing above the temp workspace; since the
workspace has no .git, the correct non-git-repo path is exercised.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use Promise<Response> for async kit handler return types (#2966)

* fix: use Promise<Response> return type for async kit handlers

When svelte-check adds type annotations to SvelteKit route handlers
(GET, POST, etc.), it was using `Response | Promise<Response>` as the
return type for all functions regardless of whether they were async.

TypeScript requires async functions to return `Promise<T>`, not
`T | Promise<T>` (TS1064). This caused false positives in
`--incremental` mode where tsc runs on the generated files directly.

The fix checks if the function has an async modifier and, if so,
extracts just the `Promise<Response>` part of the return type union.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: format sveltekit.ts with prettier

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Updated async kit handler return types to use Promise<Response>.

Apply Vamary theme design across entire site

Complete visual redesign using the Vamary theme aesthetic: warm
terracotta primary (#CD5F37), dark brown headings (#481E0B), peach and
cream backgrounds, DM Sans body font, Playfair Display heading font.
Rebuilt header (single row terracotta), footer (4-column peach), all
shared components, and all 20 pages. Square buttons, no border-radius
cards, warm color palette throughout.

Add gemspec spec tests for Gemfile.lock exclusion (#856) (#949)

## Summary

This PR adds comprehensive spec tests to verify that the gemspec
correctly excludes files from the packaged gem, particularly the
`Gemfile.lock` file which should never be included in gem distributions.

## Changes

- **Added gemspec spec suite** (`spec/shakapacker/gemspec_spec.rb`):
  - Verifies `Gemfile.lock` is excluded from `s.files`
- Verifies `spec/`, `node_modules/`, and other unwanted directories are
excluded
  - Verifies `lib/` directory and RBS type signatures are included
- Tests load the actual gemspec to ensure the file filtering logic works
correctly

- **Updated CHANGELOG.md**:
- Added entry under `[Unreleased]` documenting the gemspec fix for
excluding `Gemfile.lock`

## Testing

All tests pass:
```
bundle exec rspec spec/shakapacker/gemspec_spec.rb
5 examples, 0 failures
```

Fixes #856

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Small packaging/test-only change; it only affects which files are
included in the released gem and adds regression coverage.
> 
> **Overview**
> Updates `shakapacker.gemspec` file filtering so excluded paths match
both directories and standalone files (notably `Gemfile.lock`),
preventing the lockfile from being shipped in the built gem.
> 
> Adds a new RSpec suite (`spec/shakapacker/gemspec_spec.rb`) that loads
the gemspec and asserts key include/exclude behavior (e.g., excludes
`spec/` and `node_modules/`, includes `lib/` and `.rbs`), and documents
the fix in `CHANGELOG.md`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
d7645b2854f89be46fc7a327349e91379fbbfac4. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

commit.message:
fix: wire mobileHeroImage into getHomepageData, remove temp endpoint

The mobileHeroImage field was added to Homepage global and cms-types
but never included in the getHomepageData() return object, so the
Hero component always fell back to the main image for mobile.

repository.description: Bilingual (EN/RU) private tour portal — Next.js 15 + Payload CMS 3.x

commit.message:
fix: prerender waits for React to actually render, not just fallback title

Previously waitForFunction passed immediately because index.html has a
fallback <title> tag — React Helmet never got a chance to update it.
Now waits for #root to have children, then waits for title to change
away from the fallback before capturing HTML.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
